skill.name: blog-creator
skill.description: Create SEO-optimized MDX blog posts with required frontmatter, tags, image paths, and publishing conventions.
skill.summary: Draft structured MDX blog posts for a Next.js-style content workflow.
skill.author: AayushBaniya2006

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found those were false positives from Markdown fences, inline path formatting, and MDX-related text. No prompt injection, network access, credential handling, executable scripts, or malicious intent was found. The remaining risk is low because the skill instructs agents to create MDX files and optionally add image assets in a project.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged locations are Markdown code fences, inline paths, and examples for blog content structure. They do not invoke a shell, Ruby backticks, or any command runner.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files are Markdown documentation only, and the flagged text is formatting or path guidance. I found no shell execution primitive or generated script.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain blog frontmatter descriptions, dates, and Markdown or MDX terms. They do not define or use weak cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files contain no crypto library calls, hash functions, encryption routines, or credential-processing logic. The static signal appears to come from ordinary content terms.

low_findings.title: Project File Creation Guidance Requires Normal Review
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for a low-risk filesystem capability. The skill instructs users to create MDX blog files and optionally add images, which is expected but should remain limited to the project content directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow directly tells the agent to create a new blog file and optionally add images. This is legitimate for the skill, but it is still a filesystem write capability.

content.value_statement: Publishing MDX blog posts often requires consistent frontmatter, image paths, tags, and metadata. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create structured posts that fit the existing blog engine.
actual_capabilities: Creates MDX blog drafts with title, tags, featured image, created date, and description fields.
Uses the expected content path for blog posts under src/content/blog.
Explains how filenames become blog slugs through kebab-case naming.
Guides image placement and absolute asset paths for public images.
Applies Markdown structure with headings, introduction text, and sections.
Documents SEO metadata usage for titles, descriptions, and featured images.
limitations: It does not generate or optimize images by itself.
It assumes the host project uses the documented MDX blog engine.
It does not validate the post through a build or lint command.
It does not provide topic research or factual verification on its own.

use_cases.title: Publish a Product Update
use_cases.description: Create a formatted MDX post for a release note, feature launch, or company update.

use_cases.title: Standardize Blog Submissions
use_cases.description: Turn a draft article into the required frontmatter and Markdown structure before review.

use_cases.title: Add Developer Tutorials
use_cases.description: Create tutorial posts that follow the site slug, tag, and image conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Post
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one new article.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an MDX blog post about [topic]. Use a clear title, 3 to 5 tags, today's date, and a short SEO description.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn existing notes into the required blog format.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this draft into a blog post for the MDX content engine. Add frontmatter, improve headings, and suggest a kebab-case filename: [draft text].

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Launch Article
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a launch blog post for [product or feature]. Include SEO metadata, relevant tags, a featured image path, and a concise introduction.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Content Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a structured post aligned with site conventions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete MDX article for [audience] about [topic]. Include frontmatter, slug recommendation, section outline, SEO description, and image path guidance.

output_examples.input: Create a getting-started article for a SaaS starter kit.
output_examples.output: A complete MDX-ready article with metadata, a recommended slug, setup-focused sections, relevant tags, and a featured image path.

output_examples.input: Turn release notes into a blog announcement.
output_examples.output: A polished announcement post with a concise summary, launch context, feature sections, SEO description, and publishing metadata.

output_examples.input: Format my tutorial draft for the blog engine.
output_examples.output: A cleaned tutorial structure with frontmatter, consistent headings, corrected image references, and publication-ready Markdown.

best_practices: Keep filenames in kebab-case so generated slugs are predictable.
Use 3 to 5 focused tags that match related article logic.
Review all MDX content before publishing, especially imported components and links.
anti_patterns: Do not use single quotes in frontmatter when the project expects double quotes.
Do not place image files outside the documented public asset directory.
Do not publish generated factual claims without editorial review.

faq.question: What type of blog posts does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates MDX blog posts with frontmatter, tags, image paths, dates, descriptions, and Markdown sections.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it add images to a post?
faq.answer: It can guide image placement and references, but it does not create image assets by itself.

faq.question: Does it run build or validation commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill documentation does not define any command execution or automated validation step.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only low risk from normal project file creation guidance.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing?
faq.answer: Users should review metadata accuracy, source claims, image paths, tags, and any MDX components.
